reviews.review_text:
A timeless masterpiece

To those interested in souls games, this is the purest souls experience

Playing this game evokes an indescribable feeling recreated by no other title

I rate this game 10/10. I feel grossly incandescent, engaged in jolly cooperation, did a lil PVP, and overall having a great time. I feel like there's a few parts where people may find it frustrating. My advice? Pump some vitality and endurance; slap on heavy armor and swing for the fences with an ultra weapon, its fun. <3

This game is an absolute masterpiece. I just beat NG+ and i have to say that this is one of the best games i've ever played in my life. It is painful at the beginning and the first time i beat the game i didn't enjoy it as much i as did playing it the second time. (My 2nd playthrough i played all the bosses without using my shield). The DLC bosses are (imo) the best bosses in the whole game. (imo) the best boss in the original game is Four Kings and the DLC it's Knight Artorias

Dark Souls: REMASTERED is a masterpiece. Even tough the mechanics feel a bit clanky for newer players you'll love this game sooner or later. The whole atmosphere and setting of the game truly are magnificent. While the lore is only told in small bits, one can puzzle it together by just progressing trought the game. I would recommend this to everyone who likes a bit of an challenging game. The bosses feel sometimes a bit easy but thats because it's already collected some dust. The integreated DLC is so much worth playing as i puts up quite a challenge.

Praise the sun!  \[T]/

Dark Souls is as close to a perfect package of an RPG game as you can get. The first playthrough you get to meet all the characters and hear their stories, through them you are shown the tragedy of the world. Stories of optimism of getting to explore this world reflect on your own experience as you progress through the game. Your character gradually gets stronger, you find better weapons and armor and slowly settle in to your kind of archetype. Exploring becomes so enjoyable because there are as many pleasant surprises as there are unpleasant ones.

Once you start to get settled into the world you see these characters that you have saved and helped along the way start to assist you on your own journey. You start to realize that you could have just as easily been trapped like any of the people you saved along the way, so having this mutual give-and-take relationship with the NPCs feels earned rather than expected. It hits much harder once you progress through their storylines and slowly realize that you can't save all of them.

In terms of actual gameplay, the real-time combat is amazing. I have to say I wish aiming/using spells and bows was a bit more intuitive, but you quickly learn what tools to use in certain situations. Online multiplayer feels very authentic, you will have just as many good experiences as bad ones. Knowing that you can invade other players to harm them or be summoned to assist them makes the multiplayer experience fit perfectly into the world, you can effectively choose an "evil" or "good" path except your choices impact real people which is simply incredible.

I played two very different characters and had two complete experiences in a total of around 40 hours, so in terms of the time investment this game requires I would say 20+ hours for a single playthrough is a good expectation. Each of those 20+ hours is uniquely fun, with their own challenges and solutions to make it feel like you've figured out how to reliably complete certain zones / encounters.

Overall a true 10/10 game and experience. Not a bloated 100+ hour time sink, and not a game relying on memes to entertain you for a few hours.

Dark Souls Remastered stands as a masterclass in game design and remains one of the most influential action RPGs ever created. As my first Souls-like experience, it completely exceeded my expectations and provided an unforgettable journey from beginning to end. Despite its reputation for difficulty, I found myself so immersed in its world, lore, and gameplay that I completed the game in only four days.

One of the game's greatest strengths is its exceptional boss roster. Nearly every encounter feels unique, challenging, and rewarding, with the late-game bosses being particularly memorable. The final boss pushed my skills to their limits and required patience, perseverance, and mastery of the game's mechanics. While I struggled significantly, the eventual victory delivered an unparalleled sense of accomplishment that few games can replicate.

The interconnected world design, atmospheric environments, and deep combat system combine to create an experience that constantly rewards curiosity and determination. Having friends guide and coach me throughout my playthrough added another layer of enjoyment, making every victory feel even more satisfying.

Overall, Dark Souls Remastered is a phenomenal game that perfectly balances challenge and reward. It is a timeless classic that I would highly recommend to both newcomers and veterans of the genre. A truly legendary experience.

Genuinely loved this game so much. Everything from the visuals to the music just felt special. There were so many moments where I kinda forgot I was even playing a game and just sat there taking everything in. It honestly felt more like experiencing a piece of art than anything else. Definitely one of those games that sticks with you after you finish it.

Dark Souls 1 definitely shows its age sometimes and can feel a little clunky compared to the newer games, but honestly that never stopped it from being amazing to me. Exploring Lordran for the first time is something I genuinely don’t think I’ll forget. It’s difficult, weird, lonely, and sometimes frustrating, but that’s also what makes it fun.

7/10
First time playing this game in 2026, and I was surprised by how much there is to explore. The early game was definitely a struggle and annoying at times, but once I started finding better weapons, the experience quickly became much more enjoyable. There are plenty of hidden paths and bosses, which is exactly what I love about this style of gameplay—being free to explore wherever your curiosity takes you. Some locations are very difficult to find and easy to miss, so I ended up searching online for help, and you’ll probably have to as well. Even though this is a remaster, the graphics are badly dated. I particularly really disliked how the weight system works in the game, but aside from that, everything else was a blast to experience. Dark Souls is definitely aimed more toward experienced players because of how little guidance it gives you. I ended up happy with this playthrough, and I would recommend it to others as well.

Its a very good game.
I could write multiple paragraphs about it but instead ill just tell you to buy it and when you do dont you dare go hollow.

One of the greatest pieces of media ever created.

Dark Souls is not just one of the greatest games ever made, it is one of the greatest artistic achievements in gaming as a medium.
First of all, the aspect I love the most about souls games is themes underneath everything. The philosophy of Dark Souls is handled masterfully because the game never spoon feeds anything to the player. It trusts your intelligence. Themes about the cycle of fire and dark, fear of change, decay of civilizations, humanity, death, purpose, obsession and existential struggle are hidden beneath the surface instead of being directly explained through endless exposition. The game allows the player to interpret everything in their own way, and that makes the experience deeply personal. Very few games respect the player's imagination and intelligence like this.

The world design is genuinely legendary. Lordran feels less like a video game map and more like a real collapsing kingdom built upon forgotten ages. Every location carries immense emotional and thematic weight through architecture, atmosphere, enemy placement and environmental storytelling. Areas like Firelink Shrine, Anor Londo, New Londo Ruins, Ash Lake, Oolacile and the Kiln of the First Flame are unforgettable not because the game constantly tells you they are important, but because you FEEL their history while exploring them. The interconnected level design is still one of the greatest achievements in gaming history. Realizing how all these areas connect together naturally without artificial hand holding was genuinely mind blowing.

The bosses overall may not consistently reach the mechanical heights of Dark Souls 3, but when Dark Souls peaks, it PEAKS 🔥.... Artorias and Manus, Father of the Abyss genuinely feel ahead of their time and closest to the intensity and emotional energy of DS3 bosses.

And then there is Sif. Rescuing baby Sif in the Chasm of the Abyss before fighting Great Grey Wolf Sif in the present timeline completely changed that boss fight emotionally. When Sif recognizes the player before the fight begins, the entire battle stops feeling like a normal boss encounter and becomes tragic. One of the most emotionally powerful moments I have experienced in gaming.

The soundtrack is a masterpiece. Gwyn, Lord of Cinder’s piano theme alone is enough to prove how deeply this game understands melancholy and endings. Instead of epic bombastic music, the final fight is filled with sadness and exhaustion. It perfectly captures the soul of Dark Souls.

This game is brutal, lonely, mysterious, beautiful, tragic and unforgettable.

100/10. One Of The greatest experiences you can have in this lifetime.

First time playing through. Amazing. I can see why it has earned its reputation. Hard but doable...the game basically teaches you to get better so don't be afraid to give it a try. Grab it during a sale. I started with Elden Ring but switched to this early. Going to play them all now and can't wait to eventually get back to Elden Ring to see where the franchise has gone.
I think i need a very old japanese man to play this and write a haiku about what it has taught him and what this game means to him. That is the way the lore was intended to be exprienced right there.
run two minutes to get to bed of chaos, get slapped to oblivion, run another two minutes just to fail the jump. 10/10

10/10 would roll off a cliff by accident again

I first played Dark Souls 1 on ps3 back in 2012 and since then have played it to completion so many times on different platforms. Over time, I've easily accumulated over 1K hours of playtime across those platforms and I would do it all over again without hesitation. If you have any interest in getting into the souls series, then this is where I recommend you start. 

Paise the sun! \[T]/
Good game, even though it features kinda frustrating levels, such as invisible paths, swarms of enemies comming from nowhere, GRAVITY. I had a good time playing it.

Would definitely recommend anyone to try this, not as fast paced as DS3 or as much spammy stuff as in Elden Ring, going back to the roots... it was quite enjoyable, I only played this after DS3+ER+Sekiro and glad I did.

It's definitely got it's jank and I honestly didn't love it on my first playthrough after coming from Elden Ring but after replaying it again to get to the DLC I had a much greater appreciation for this game. Great music and a maze like map that had me so lost on my first playthrough that I was getting more frustrated at the level design than the difficulty of the bosses themselves. This wasn't an issue on the second playthrough however and I can see myself playing this game again just to try out new weapons and to experience the world all over again

A unique European medieval adventure with the philosophical style of Hidetaka Miyazaki. And this is completely different from those talentless game developers nowadays. The challenges in its game design are essentially about honing skills in puzzle-solving and exploring combat rhythms, not like those studios nowadays that just pile up a mountain of crappy code to make things hard for the sake of difficulty. Sure, Elden Ring also has some bad coding for reading commands inputs, and bosses like Consort of Miquella are terrible both story-wise and mechanically. But that’s another story. FromSoftware hadn't forgotten its roots when this work came out.

Nary is there a game more deserving of the moniker "Classic" than this. While there's so much that has already been said about this game, the level design in the first half can never be praised enough, gradually exploring and unlocking shortcuts that connect the map together gives a sense of progression and the feeling of how far you've come like nothing else.
phenomenal, one of the most satisfying and emotional gaming experiences ive ever had

This game came to me at a very stressful time in my life. What was first a frustratingly complicated challenge became an unexpected method of coping through mental and emotional struggles I have been battling with.

I didn't know much about Dark Souls before playing it. I only knew of the usual frame this game is painted in: it's difficult, but rewarding. The lore is deep and complex. It will make you want to rip your hair out. I knew all these things going in, but I never expected it to become such an important thing in my life.

No game in my entire lifetime of playing them has ever left such a profound impact on me like Dark Souls has. I'm not sure if any game ever will. This is a beautifully crafted experience that helped me save myself from my own personal abyss. I couldn't be more grateful to have played this game from beginning to end. I love it so much. Please play it.
This game was pretty peak, got two playthroughs done. I will say that this game very much holds up :]

One of the best experiences I've ever had with a game. If you can set your expectations realistically, and get past the 2nd half of the game, this will be a really good time. Yes, the game is hard, but it's also very simple and easy to understand once you know the controls. Definitely a must buy for sure.
Had already played and beaten it multiple times on xbox. This game is THE MASTERPIECE. The music and setting just bring you into a new world. Praise the sun!

"Is dark souls good?" Is like asking if my wife would stop cheating on me. The answer is yes, if you are willing to forgive. The lore is simply poetry and the world is beautifully crafted. I bought this game for Xbox and steam so there seems to be a few differences but I feel like they are obvious. Looks better on pc, less cheaters on console. I wouldn't let cheaters ruin my experience though or deter the fact that I've bought the same game multiple times so hopefully it doesn't with others either. I've really only ran into one but other comments with more playtime make it seem more common than it is. Nonetheless, the characters they have are some of the best. Whether it means dying to save your best friend or being driven mad by a parasite while trying to shine some light on such a dark world. I just wish there was more cutscenes that explains it better because you have to search and pay attention otherwise you might not know that you offer the souls to Gwyn when you level up or other aspects of the lore. Captivating poetry that you can explore is the best way to describe the entire series and highly recommend for anyone trying to "Git Gud".  --BogDog
if the game isnt ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ enough itll sometimes not support your controller

As a new comer, DS1 can be a bit rough around the edges, however it’s still an extremely well made and fun game. The world design (for the first half) is extremely well made and fun to traverse. The combat more often than not is engaging and really makes you focus on what is the most efficient way to do things. Building your character and finding new weapons and armor is extremely fun for someone like me. There are many small problems with it, but overall as my first fromsoft soulslike, this definitely hooked me on the series and will definitely bring playing 2, 3, and Elden Ring.
The first game was great.......this game is better by leaps and miles

I never would have picked this game for myself but my friend was absolutely positive I'd love it and...  what an amazing experience it was.  If I could wipe my own memories and play it again anew I would.

I see how people like this game, especially if they played it as their first souls game. I think it was alright. After playing DS3 first it just felt clunky and like it had a lot of 'interesting' design decisisions. I'm glad I  played it and if souls likes are something you think you'd like then go ahead, and just know it gets better at least in DS3.

I like this happy little game. its has taught me a lot about patience and how to to not overreact when a giant dragon crushes your little knight for the millionth time just because you really want to see what weapon his tail turns into

I have played this game three times in total when on different platforms the 1st was on my day 1 release switch the second was on my pre ordered xbox series x and finally on steam and every time I have loved this game and have 100% it each time since this game had introduced me to good games and imo is well worth playing even if it's not to completion like me but just try it that's all I would say and if you are coming from Elden Ring play either this or Demon Souls to show what was the start of such a brilliant string of games and their own genre

the only parts of the game i have left are the dlc final boss and the base final boss, so i think i have enough to make an informed, carefully crafted and methodical review on this game.

was good :D

edit: ok but fr, fun but wayyyy less challenging than folks made it out to be. i was shocked to learn people find O&S and astorius to be super hard when they're pretty easy to play around (dex build hehe). maybe im a poor judge of difficulty bc i died more times to asylum demon and its variants lol

edit again: just beat manus. is this really the guy people said was impossible????? it was so easy (i guess the complaint comes from str players with heavy roll and windup?)
Instant classic. Would greatly recommend if you enjoyed games like hollow knight, or other similar games.

Obviously, it's incredibly hard, but playable and highly rewarding. While sometimes being a bit too slow, it's a great test of patience and timing. It's brilliant learning how to anticipate and avoid particular attacks that are troubling a fight, and once the solution clicks, it renews hope, granting just enough strength to go a bit further, hopefully to the endgame.
Best fromsoft world design by far. Save Solaire so you can clap the Lord of Cinder together as bros

I'm so glad this was my gateway into Soul- borne games.
If you can embrace the difficulty and accept you will fail multiple times, it's the most rewarding experience.

I had completed the original game 100% on Xbox 360, and was happy to do it again with the remastered version. The DLC is included with the game, which is a great side quest where you may learn more about Sif and Artorias. The online community is thriving, I found myself invaded frequently in popular areas like Anor Londo and the Undead Parish. This also allowed me to be summoned by other players, and to bring other players into help for boss fights. The 100% achievement guide posted by Cynic 0055 on steam is helpful for those looking to obtain 100% of the achievements.

I played the game with a controller, and a headset to fully enjoy the soundtrack and sound effects. If you enjoy the souls series, make sure to pick this up.
Great gramphics great grameplay. Second souls game and exceeded my expectations, accidently picked the ending and went straight in to NG+. 10/10

I'll start my review with saying I have completed three 100% runs for this game and everyone of them was Hell and a Blessing. These games will teach you determination, resolve, struggle, and it will reward you along the way. The boss battles in this game are Iconic to the Souls series as a whole and are unforgiving. This game doesn't have flashy jump attacks, crazy dodge frames, and it definitely won't hold your hand. Get yourself a good guide and try one of the most iconic video games to hit screens!

Phenomenal game -- unforgiving for the sake of being rewarding.

10/10 would do again
This game is really great..I played it on steamdeck and it runs so well..Right now I'm a fan of souls like games..

Dear Hidetaka Miyazaki,

Thank you for approving the Dark Souls Remaster so I could experience peak suffering on modern hardware.

This was my first soulslike. Five minutes in, I already understood that this game was either going to become one of the greatest experiences of my life or send me into a medically concerning mental state. Somehow it did both. During my playthrough I sacrificed 2 controllers, 1 keyboard, my remaining sanity. My monitor survived several incoming objects and earned my respect. Everyone in my house also learned that I apparently know insults in languages I don’t even speak.

Dark Souls taught me an important lesson: no matter how many times life beats you down, you can always stand back up and launch Stardew Valley instead.

I wholeheartedly recommend this game to anyone who has been neglecting their hygiene and mental stability for months — you will truly feel at home here.

10/10 masterpiece. I will never recover from this experience.
it plays like swimming through peanut butter, the second half of the game is eh, the connectivity is cool, you learn to appreciate what you have now. try it
The best dark fantasy rpg I have ever played. It trolls you in some parts but thats what makes it fun.

This is a masterpiece of a game, especially for the time it was created, but it's still good today. If you're coming from elden ring, just know this game is 100x more clunky, but it'll feel nice to get used to it (trust me). The bossfights are great, the areas are very memorable, the world structure is beyond impressive. Overall much worth your money if you're interested in the souls style games.

Just got done with getting 100% on both prepare to die edition and this, and I have to say, Dark souls is still one of if not the best game still, at least in my opinion. I can't wait to move onto getting 100% on Dark souls 2 and 3 next.

Depois de muitos anos estou jogando novamente Dark Souls 1, só que agora em sua versão remasterizada sem as limitações do port anterior que se não estou enganado era do XBOX 360. Nos limitava a 30 FPS, tinha muitos bugs e falhas nas comunicação multiplayer, nesse parece que tudo foi resolvido. Antigamente usei mods para ficar jogável. Dito isso, o jogo tem suas limitações mas ainda continua muito bonito e dando muita raiva quando morremos, especialmente porque aqui o teleporte entre fogueiras é limitado, mas quando avançamos na história as coisas vão melhorando.

Finally finishing this game after half a year makes me upset I couldn't get into the Souls games sooner because I was intimidated by its difficulty. Played DS3 in 2020 but dropped it for that reason and now I have regrets. I'm starting from the beginning of the trilogy after beating Elden Ring and I've come to appreciate these games more and more.

With the advent of Elden Ring, and the many clones that have come out, it is without a doubt not lost on me to recognize the value from Dark Souls and Demon Souls as the precursors. I've owned the base game and the PTD edition many times over my life, but like The Elder Scrolls Skyrim, i find myself getting another version of this very special game. The narrative is loose, but ties itself together in atmosphere and worldbuilding. The combat may be clunky at times, but commitment is the name of the game. I appreciate DS3's updates to the crafting and building paths in hindsight though.

Either way, I'd say its a beautiful game with near infinite replay value.

Steps to Dark Souls Remastered:
1.Play the game
2.Rage quit for a month
3.Come back and finally beat the game

This game feels like a must play, the level design is immediately iconic and the characters are memorable. The gameplay is obviously genre defining and that is no exception with this first release of the dark souls franchise. From Software nailed it
Best map design, exploration experience, and story in the Dark Souls series, in my opinion. Although the game is dated, some of the boss fights are still fun and challenging.
now i thought this game was gonna be really hard its just hard harder than elden ring but if you put the work in your chilling
the reputation for these games are pretty spot on with its difficulty, allthough it just makes the game all the more sweeter. cant wait to try out later installments (not sure about dark souls 2 doe)

very good game. Unfortunately peaks at sens fortress in terms of level design. Anor londo itself was as disappointing maze of empty sterile hallways with the saving grace being ornstein and smough. Post-lordvessel content is blatantly unfinished and riddled with unfun gimmicks, the whole "collect 4 souls to fill the lordvessel" is not only tiring but also lacks the mystery and wonder of ringing the two bells and ascending to anor londo. All in all very good game brought down by the second half.

Super fun and unique experience. I haven't finished the game yet at the time of this comment, but I love this game so much. It can be pretty difficult at times, but that is what makes it so fun. Dark Souls 1 is definitely my favorite in the series. 10/10 for me.

Dark Souls is my favourite cozy game.

I've put in an obscene amount of hours on this game between consoles and now PC. It has a steep learning curve when you first begin, but once you get a hang of the gameplay mechanics, there is nothing else like it. Dark Souls remains among my favourite games due to it's combat and build system (want to be a healer and bonk enemies on the head with a gigantic sword? Go ahead!), level design and connectivity, boss fights, and characters. 

There are still some cons as well;
- some gameplay mechanics are not well explained (such as weapon scaling and tiers)
- the camera colliding with objects causing your character to veer off ledges
- the entire Demon Ruins to Bed of Chaos run feels lazy and rushed
- lack of omni-directional rolling

Overall, a 10/10 experience every time. Not even having to face bonewheel skeletons 100 times over could make me hate it (at least, not forever).

At first I did not get it; but now I do.

Stick with it... block, roll & embrace the lore.

You will be converted.

I think this just might be the pinnacle of level design well at least in the first half of the game,an excellent soundtrack and has a fantastic bosses(manus,ornstein&smough,artorias),second half definitely had a big drop in quality but none that is too bad except for lost izalith,before this the only souls game I have played is sekiro and from these two I have played I'll certainly play more

A really great game with an amazing map designe and interesting but difficult bosses. Well the game is all around preaty difficult so you will have to think of diffrent ways to get through certain locations. The combat is kinda simple and not so difficult to master since you press like on or two buttons but that didn´t really bother me cause there is a very wide asset of weapons. Exploration is very incentivised so that you can find new gear, spells and key items. If you are strugelling I would recommend using a shield or using spells since they are very powerfull. And if you get lost just look up some guide if you really don/t know how to progress. I would recommend this game to players who are looking for a chalange or for those wanting to explore a unique map and uncover the secrets of this wretched and hopeless world. Also use anything at your disposel since this game is very unforgiving and try not to break any of your furniture after dying to the bosses runback. It is also quite janky and the second halve after acquering the soul vessel is preatty dissapointing especially Lost Izalith (The whole lava area might be the soriest level in this game and is just god awful). But still probably my favourite game in the series (except for bloodborne) just because of the map.
8,5/10
I mean I recommend it if, you're up for a challenge, because it sure is a hard game if you've never played a souls like.

This is a wonderful game with fun combat and an amazing world to explore, the first half of the game is amazing with fun and challenging level design with pretty good bosses overall, the second half of the game is where it shows its cracks but still overall this is an amazing game.

It's Dark Souls. What else can I say? It is pretty scummy that Bamco sold us the patch with slightly worse lighting for a completely broken game for 20 bucks and delisted the original. Either way, this is still a fantastic way to play the game.

amazing story and gameplay that makes you want to stop living
11/10 game
its hard and the second half falls off but it is one of the most rewarding games ever

Its clanky, its slow, its dark. Yet one of the best games I have ever played, filling me with nostalgia. You can and you will be lost, even if you remember where you are going. The occasional "what? HOW?!" moments will fill you with dread and unexpected surprises. You can ruin an entire run by selecting the wrong YES/NO answer just because you get confused what they expect. When you smash your gamepad into the monitor out of frustration, you know you became a true fan. Never change Dark Souls, never change...
10/10 classic game, arguably one of the best games of all time. However it is a little too easy tbh.

DS1 is a true must play for any new or old Fromsoft fan. (I'm replaying the series for 100% achievements)
While the game definitely shows its age in areas or certain mechanics there is just a sort of magic that DS1 captures which all other titles after don't quite fully grasp. very simple gameplay compared to later titles and especially Elden Ring, everything you do is much more "intentional" and carries some form of weight behind it, weather it's the swing of your weapon or the choice to go down that ominous looking hallway all those little choices add up and can either pay off big or come back to bite you. The level design and atmosphere in this game are unmatched by not just souls games but nearly any game ever, the choice to not have warping at the start is a strong one but also an incredible one for the player as it literally forces actual exploration and encourages you to learn the gorgeous world that they crafted.. and before you know it, that silent ambience turns into a somber violin as you return to your sanctuary at Firelink shrine, the decision to have 0 ost playing outside of bosses or Firelink was absolutely beautiful and really gave those moments impact. The bosses in DS1 range from "wow this thing is super cool" to "wtf is this, why am I doing this to myself" but thankfully they lean mostly on the decent side with a few really fun and impressive outliers even by today standards, definitely not the worst roster in the series but far from the best of the best as well. I wont even get into the story or I'd be writing out an entire book but it's quite well known by now that the souls games have some of the most compelling and lore driven stories ever and I think everyone should get to engage with it in some form. 

TLDR: Play DS1, it's old but genuine gold. 
solid 8/10

The world is so interconnected and fun to explore that I wish I had played it sooner. Plenty of weapons and spells to experiment and craft cool builds with, even if all my characters end up using the claymore to bash in heads.

Wow first i would like to say how immersive this game is unless your dad left you and you play on max brightness.

Great world building and love the fact that the game just let you figure out where to go and what to do.

DLC was ahh right and the base game is REALLY GOOD! 

7.8 / 10

It's Dark Souls. If you don't know what it is by now, this game may not be for you. If you enjoy coming home from a hard day's work and relax by playing video games, you may want to reconsider purchasing this game. However, if you enjoy coming home from work and slamming your head into a wall over and over again, by all means give this game a try. More or less, this game will teach you trial and error. It'll teach you patience. It'll teach you how to pick your moment and when to execute. This game will make you want to tear your ears off of your skull and throw your controller through your monitor. This game will also provide you with dopamine the likes of which substances could never produce. Always kill the dogs first. Buy Purple Moss and Homeward Bones. Never level Resistance. The Thief STARTS with the Master Key, so select Black Firebombs to take out the first boss as soon as you see him. Locking on isn't always your friend. Get a 100% Physical Reduction shield and keep it with you at all times. Getting invaded is a honor, not a hinderance. You will come to truly understand what it means to conquer a video game upon completion. Good luck. Don't go hollow, and don't give up, Skeleton. Praise the Sun.

Ahhh, Dark Souls, my beloved. Since I decided to make the switch to PC permanent, I just have to replay the Fromsoft games.

Where to begin. The game is not very large by today's standards, but boy does it feel rich in every other way. The level design is sublime, the gameplay and mechanics are logical, smart and unique. The boss designs and lore give you goosebumps. This is the type of game you really need to play to experience it. The eureka moments when you find a shortcut, or hidden treasure is something else in this game. It really gives you the feeling of accomplishment when you learn to overcome in this very dark and lonely world.
which makes you appreciate all the more the little sparkles of warmth and comfort every once in a while.

This all might be my nostalgia speaking, but by all means, if you like classic, more slow-paced games, which embrace the core essence of what gaming was all about in the early days, give it a go!

Good luck! And don't you dare go hollow, friend!
Relembrando o momento que entrei no mundo Souls e nunca mais sai...muito bom jogar novamente.
The amount of "Fatty" messages I found is impressive, left some myself, 10/10. Greatest messaging system since the telegraph.

Dark Souls 1, what a fantastic game that was!

From having played all the three Dark Souls games, for the first time, back-to-back, this year…
This one, surely was my favorite. Even at its lowest points, (in terms of fun, that is— Shoutout to the invisible pathways in Crystal Cave), the experience in the end was well worth it!

And turns out I kind of got addicted to the combat (like a lot), specifically this one… that I’ve always came around to do “just another run”, and tried a different build, every time. Sure, later entries offered more fluid and faster combat (like DS3 and Elden Ring) … but I don’t know… I feel like DS1 just rewarded the various builds players came up with. And one thing I really enjoyed as well was that I didn’t need to constantly roll away, waiting for an enemy or boss to end their combo-attack. Heck, I felt like my armor, (the poise stat too), mattered a lot more in this game.

But the real gold… for me, was the world design. Man, I was blown away when I started playing for the first time and realized that my efforts and struggles led me back to one very familiar place, after clearing the Undead Burg/reaching Undead Parish. And yet, that is just the most basic example.

I’m very glad I was nudged to play this game, via a random video on the internet. 
It led me to play the rest of the series, (and Elden Ring), but I know down the line… I’ll be back for another trip in Lordran. 

(Say 'Hi' to Gwyn for me if you see him).

There's something very special about the mood and allusive narrative structure of FromSoftware games. Coming from Elden Ring which I greatly liked as well, I really enjoyed finally playing the game that started it all. It did not disappoint.

In fact, in many ways the game is much better. Here, combat is intuitive, reactive, and physically realistic. No skinny enemy holding their sword at maximum extension for 1.5 seconds before a delayed hit to throw your dodge off. You and your enemies fight as could be expected based on positioning and body movement. This allows you to genuinely "git gud" at the game, not at learning patterns. To artificially increase the difficulty for players who enjoy rote memorization over intuitive combat flow, and who have way too much time on their hands to learn every boss' movement set and relish that this will gatekeep the game better, later games would sadly do away with this. 

The first half is universally stellar. The second has a few weaker points to my taste with some areas feeling almost unfinished (like Ash Lake). It doesn't change that it's an amazing game I'll instantly start a second run on once I'll now take on the final boss.

I've heard much about Dark Souls and its phenomenon. At the time it came out, I was preoccupied with mmorpgs and rts games so I didn't really pay it much attention. As someone who grew up with gaming  since the 80's and went in to DS from scratch & completely blind, this is basically "Castlevania 3D". Puzzles, dungeons, interconnected environments, goth aesthetics...

Dark Souls is an adventure action-RPG game with a traditional design that was different than the more loose, player-friendly, cinematic-based games of the 2010s. Games from the 1980's were more-or-less like this. These types of games did not explicitly tell the player what to do and had stricter rules in how they were made and how people were expected to play them. This gives them the impression of being "difficult". Like any adventure game, trial-and-error were expected. In Dark Souls, the interconnected environments are a pleasure, especially after clearing a long area and its end boss. Some puzzles had more than one solution which I thought was nice. The combat...ehhh...too slow. The item design is very well done. Each item serves a purpose, even the ones you would think as "worthless". 

New players, especially those accustomed to more player-friendly games, will struggle with the Souls series. This, I will say, is the biggest flaw of Dark Souls. It is not welcoming by design. This is a type of game that does not explicitly tell the player what to do and expects them to be patient and follow its rules. It hinges on the player's personality.

Overall, a decent game!

great game with a lot of charm. it can feel very jank at times due to its age and Remaster has brought in some very easy to use exploits, but overall it is probably my favorite of the trilogy.

Playing Dark Souls today definitely shows its age in some areas. The movement can feel a little stiff compared to modern action RPGs, certain boss runs are frustrating, and some mechanics aren’t always explained clearly. Combat is slower and less polished than later entries, so new players may need some patience adjusting to it.

That said, the game’s strengths are still incredible even years later. The world design is one of the best in gaming. Exploring Lordran feels like uncovering a real, interconnected kingdom where every shortcut, hidden path, and landmark matters. Areas loop back into each other in ways that still feel satisfying and memorable.

The NPCs are another standout. Characters like Solaire of Astora and Siegmeyer of Catarina leave a lasting impression because they feel mysterious, tragic, and human despite how little direct exposition the game gives you. Their stories reward players who pay attention.

The lore is where the game truly shines. Instead of dumping information on you, Dark Souls lets players piece together its history through item descriptions, environmental storytelling, and cryptic dialogue. That approach creates a sense of mystery that makes the world feel ancient and meaningful.

Even with dated gameplay elements, the atmosphere, lore, NPC writing, and world design make it a masterpiece that still deserves its legendary reputation.

i started with Elden ring haven't completed it yet but wanted to take break from that so i thought to play DS1 overall Good game, excellent lore most of the bosses are easy if it's not your first soul game 100 percent recommended if you don't have any personal life and you hate yourself

After finishing Dark Souls, I feel like I'll never see video games the same again. The experience is truly humbling. The Elder Scrolls series are one of my favorites, as well as Dark Messiah and Arx Fatalis, so I really like fantasy games.

But, after playing this gem of a game, I realized just how spoiled I was in terms of video game difficulty. Also, it opened my eyes to what is possible within video game design, as if I escaped the matrix. The interconnected world of Dark Souls, the lack of fast travel to every single location, so many different play styles, it's really refreshing.

This game doesn't hold your hand, in fact it slaps it away and kicks you off the ledge. Dungeons are dark, swamps are toxic and will kill you, dragons are huge and dangerous, quests are vague and the story is in a way cryptic.

I avoided playing this game for years, because I knew how difficult it was, so one day when I wanted to try and play it for the sake of at least trying it, I rage quit 5 times, died at least 50 and blamed the game for being stupidly difficult for not letting me tank through the enemies like in every other game I played. 
I kept making excuses, until I got so angry for not being able to make it that I kept trying and trying, until I finally realized that I have to change the way I have been taught to progress within a video game.

After being humbled and actually starting to learn the enemy's attack patterns, it got easier, until you meet more and more new enemies, but that's where the fun began. The accomplishment you get after defeating bosses is unlike I have ever experienced, even in late game. Like, there is this black dragon boss in the DLC and even with the best gear and skills, I still died at least 40 times. 

That being said, after finally managing to defeat the dragon, I actually shed a tear, because of the accomplishment that I felt. It was the most satisfying thing I have ever experienced in a video game.

Overcoming challenging enemies, discovering the lore through item description and exploring this vast interconnected world of Lordran is peak gaming experience.

it is hard to commit to and is grindy as ♥♥♥♥ but is addictive and I have never played a souls game so this is my first playthrough. it is hard and challenging but ill do it and complete d2 d3 and elden.

- Finally reach the boss : spanks you and sends you back home
- Try to reach the boss again through shortcut : falls in the pit , died again
- Beat the boss somehow only to repeat this again several times

But overall great game with crazy mechanics and bosses, pushing me to play the complete trilogy along with all the achievements.

And yes, be wary of fog gates XD

Dark Souls is one of the most iconic and influential RPGs ever developed. It's not perfect, in some ways it's downright janky and dirty, even for 2011 standards. However, for the foundation it laid out and the iconic gameplay it presented, I think every gamer should go through it and appreciate it for what it is: a dark medieval fantasy that forces you to earn your competence in the most disrespectful way possible.

Some people say video games are just entertainment through a medium. I can talk about the gameplay, the setting, the weapons, etc., but when you think about it, a game like Dark Souls teaches you so many life lessons. It's a game about struggling. And I think this is truly where it shines. Through struggle, you're taught patience, discipline, keeping your nerves under wraps in the heat of the moment, and how to take in and feel accomplished from both small and large victories. Most importantly, it teaches you how to get back up after a crushing defeat and persist forward. This is truly a character building video game, both in and out, and for that it's earned my respect over the past nearly 15 years.

Every gamer should play through this at least once. I know when I have a son, he'll be forced to. And if he quits, he's getting table scraps and no love. Praise the sun!
